summary: Finished conservative proof-era metadata classification for the fifteen legacy handoff records that batch-1 deferred. Backfilled proof_level, missing_proof_levels, contract_status_changes, residual_risks, repo_clean_status, git_mirror_status, and dirty_paths_outside_scope only from the original handoff and session evidence; preserved legacy-unknown semantics for repo cleanliness and mirror state where the older trail did not record them.
recommended_next_reason: No further legacy handoffs remain unclassified; proof-era evidence fields are now uniform across all automation handoff records.

contract_status_changes.before: Fifteen legacy handoffs still lacked proof-era evidence fields after batch-1: fourteen predated proof_level entirely, and one (harness-proof-level-ladder) carried proof_level and missing_proof_levels but predated the broader evidence fields added in the later owlory-handoff-evidence-writer slice.
contract_status_changes.after: All fifteen records now carry the full proof-era field set. Classifications were drawn only from each slice's own summary, validations_passed list, and recorded risks; proof claims were not upgraded based on later unrelated work. The harness-proof-level-ladder author's existing proof_level, missing_proof_levels, and dirty_paths_outside_scope were preserved verbatim.

residual_risks: Backfilled repo_clean_status is uniformly unknown and git_mirror_status uniformly not-checked because older handoffs did not assert final workspace or upstream state; that uncertainty is honest but cannot be retroactively recovered.

doc-only and build-tested classifications for slices that ran regression-guard tests against unchanged code may look conservative compared with the validations_passed list; the proof_level reflects the slice's deliverable, not the codebase-wide test run.

harness-proof-level-ladder retains its original residual_risks text noting the broader evidence fields were not added in that slice; that historical risk language is now technically resolved by this batch but was preserved verbatim per the evidence-not-confidence rule.
